content.text: The UI relaunched, but the old `bridge-server` child stayed alive as an orphan, so that was not a full clean restart. I’m doing one more pass: terminate both the app and its bridge process, relaunch the app bundle, and verify both fresh PIDs.

payload.delta: The UI relaunched, but the old `bridge-server` child stayed alive as an orphan, so that was not a full clean restart. I’m doing one more pass: terminate both the app and its bridge process, relaunch the app bundle, and verify both fresh PIDs.

entries.summary:
The macOS app was restarted cleanly.

I first relaunched the UI process, but its old `bridge-server` child stayed alive, so I killed both a...

content.text:
The macOS app was restarted cleanly.

I first relaunched the UI process, but its old `bridge-server` child stayed alive, so I killed both and started the app again from:
[/Users/lubomirmolin/Library/Developer/Xcode/DerivedData/VibeBridgeCompanion-ekndmlpbuttxivelnjaqmpmtjbjl/Build/Products/Debug/VibeBridgeCompanion.app](/Users/lubomirmolin/Library/Developer/Xcode/DerivedData/VibeBridgeCompanion-ekndmlpbuttxivelnjaqmpmtjbjl/Build/Products/Debug/VibeBridgeCompanion.app)

The fresh app PID is `90311`. At the moment of verification only the app process was up; the bridge child had not spawned yet, so if the app starts the bridge lazily, that part will come up on first use.

entries.summary: I’m tracing the thread-open path in the bridge and mobile client so I can separate network latency from expensive local sync work. Given th...

content.text: I’m tracing the thread-open path in the bridge and mobile client so I can separate network latency from expensive local sync work. Given the fixes we just made, my first suspicion is that thread open now blocks on a full upstream sync before returning the timeline.

payload.delta: I’m tracing the thread-open path in the bridge and mobile client so I can separate network latency from expensive local sync work. Given the fixes we just made, my first suspicion is that thread open now blocks on a full upstream sync before returning the timeline.

The slow part is the bridge sync on thread open, not Flutter rendering.

When the mobile detail page opens, it immediately calls `fetchThre...

content.text:
The slow part is the bridge sync on thread open, not Flutter rendering.

When the mobile detail page opens, it immediately calls `fetchThreadTimelinePage(...)` from [thread_detail_controller.dart#L275](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/vibe-bridge-companion/apps/mobile/lib/features/threads/application/thread_detail_controller.dart#L275). On the bridge, `GET /threads/:id/timeline` now does a synchronous `sync_from_upstream()` first in [lib.rs#L1636](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/vibe-bridge-companion/crates/bridge-core/src/lib.rs#L1636). That sync is expensive because `sync_from_upstream()` reloads the entire snapshot in [thread_api.rs#L151](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/vibe-bridge-companion/crates/bridge-core/src/thread_api.rs#L151), and `load_thread_snapshot(...)` in [thread_api.rs#L998](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/vibe-bridge-companion/crates/bridge-core/src/thread_api.rs#L998) does two heavyweight things:

1. It asks the Codex app-server for all threads, not just the selected one.
2. For each thread, it does `thread/read` with `includeTurns: true` in [thread_api.rs#L2378](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/vibe-bridge-companion/crates/bridge-core/src/thread_api.rs#L2378), which means full turn history.
3. Then it also reads and merges archive history from `~/.codex`.

So opening one session currently pays the cost of rebuilding history for up to 50 threads. That is the main reason it feels slow.

There are two smaller contributors:
- The mobile page now asks for a deeper first page, `80` entries, in [thread_detail_page.dart#L32](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/vibe-bridge-companion/apps/mobile/lib/features/threads/presentation/thread_detail_page.dart#L32) and [thread_detail_controller.dart#L30](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/vibe-bridge-companion/apps/mobile/lib/features/threads/application/thread_detail_controller.dart#L30).
- After the timeline returns, the page also fetches git status and starts the live stream.

The right fix is not to undo the correctness change. It is to make thread-open sync thread-scoped:
- For `/threads/:id` and `/threads/:id/timeline`, call `thread/read` only for that one thread instead of `fetch_all_threads()`.
- Merge archive only for that thread id.
- Keep the full multi-thread sync for `/threads` list only.
- Optionally serve cached timeline immediately, then refresh in background.

That would preserve the correct activity you now see while making thread open much faster. If you want, I can implement that next.

payload.delta:
The slow part is the bridge sync on thread open, not Flutter rendering.

When the mobile detail page opens, it immediately calls `fetchThreadTimelinePage(...)` from [thread_detail_controller.dart#L275](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/vibe-bridge-companion/apps/mobile/lib/features/threads/application/thread_detail_controller.dart#L275). On the bridge, `GET /threads/:id/timeline` now does a synchronous `sync_from_upstream()` first in [lib.rs#L1636](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/vibe-bridge-companion/crates/bridge-core/src/lib.rs#L1636). That sync is expensive because `sync_from_upstream()` reloads the entire snapshot in [thread_api.rs#L151](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/vibe-bridge-companion/crates/bridge-core/src/thread_api.rs#L151), and `load_thread_snapshot(...)` in [thread_api.rs#L998](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/vibe-bridge-companion/crates/bridge-core/src/thread_api.rs#L998) does two heavyweight things:

1. It asks the Codex app-server for all threads, not just the selected one.
2. For each thread, it does `thread/read` with `includeTurns: true` in [thread_api.rs#L2378](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/vibe-bridge-companion/crates/bridge-core/src/thread_api.rs#L2378), which means full turn history.
3. Then it also reads and merges archive history from `~/.codex`.

So opening one session currently pays the cost of rebuilding history for up to 50 threads. That is the main reason it feels slow.

There are two smaller contributors:
- The mobile page now asks for a deeper first page, `80` entries, in [thread_detail_page.dart#L32](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/vibe-bridge-companion/apps/mobile/lib/features/threads/presentation/thread_detail_page.dart#L32) and [thread_detail_controller.dart#L30](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/vibe-bridge-companion/apps/mobile/lib/features/threads/application/thread_detail_controller.dart#L30).
- After the timeline returns, the page also fetches git status and starts the live stream.

The right fix is not to undo the correctness change. It is to make thread-open sync thread-scoped:
- For `/threads/:id` and `/threads/:id/timeline`, call `thread/read` only for that one thread instead of `fetch_all_threads()`.
- Merge archive only for that thread id.
- Keep the full multi-thread sync for `/threads` list only.
- Optionally serve cached timeline immediately, then refresh in background.

That would preserve the correct activity you now see while making thread open much faster. If you want, I can implement that next.

content.text: I’ve got the change shape. I’m adding a thread-scoped snapshot loader and a `sync_thread_from_upstream(thread_id)` path in the bridge, then I’ll swap the detail/timeline routes over to that narrower sync. This keeps the same archive+RPC merge logic, just for one thread instead of all threads.

This keeps the same archive+RPC merge logic, just for one thread instead of all threads.

+                    headline: "Unrelated thread".to_string(),
+                    lifecycle_state: "done".to_string(),
+                    workspace_path: "/workspace/other".to_string(),
+                    repository_name: "other".to_string(),
+                    branch_name: "main".to_string(),
+                    remote_name: "origin".to_string(),
+                    git_dirty: false,
+                    git_ahead_by: 0,
+                    git_behind_by: 0,
+                    created_at: "2026-03-17T10:00:00Z".to_string(),
+                    updated_at: "2026-03-17T10:00:00Z".to_string(),
+                    source: "cli".to_string(),
+                    approval_mode: "control_with_approvals".to_string(),
+                    last_turn_summary: "other summary".to_string(),
+                },
+            ],
+            timeline_by_thread_id: HashMap::from([
+                (
+                    "thread-target".to_string(),
+                    vec![UpstreamTimelineEvent {
+                        id: "stale-target-event".to_string(),
+                        event_type: "agent_message_delta".to_string(),
+                        happened_at: "2026-03-17T10:00:00Z".to_string(),
+                        summary_text: "stale target event".to_string(),
+                        data: json!({"delta": "stale target event", "role": "assistant"}),
+                    }],
+                ),
+                (
+                    "thread-other".to_string(),
+                    vec![UpstreamTimelineEvent {

+                codex_command: "/definitely/missing/codex".to_string(),

+                codex_home: codex_home.clone(),
+            }),
+        };
+
+        service
+            .sync_thread_from_upstream("thread-target")
+            .expect("thread sync should fall back to archive");
+
+        let refreshed_target = service
+            .thread_records
+            .iter()
+            .find(|thread| thread.id == "thread-target")
+            .expect("target thread should remain present");
+        assert_eq!(refreshed_target.headline, "Fresh target title");
+        assert_eq!(refreshed_target.last_turn_summary, "Fresh target body.");
+
+        let untouched_other = service
+            .thread_records
+            .iter()
+            .find(|thread| thread.id == "thread-other")
+            .expect("other thread should remain present");
+        assert_eq!(untouched_other.headline, "Unrelated thread");
+        assert_eq!(untouched_other.last_turn_summary, "other summary");
+
+        let target_timeline = service
+            .timeline_by_thread_id
+            .get("thread-target")
+            .expect("target timeline should exist");
+        assert_eq!(target_timeline.len(), 1);
+        assert_eq!(target_timeline[0].summary_text, "Fresh target body.");
+
+        let other_timeline = service
+            .timeline_by_thread_id
+            .get("thread-other")
+            .expect("other timeline should still exist");
+        assert_eq!(other_timeline.len(), 1);
+        assert_eq!(other_timeline[0].id, "other-event");
+
+        let _ = fs::remove_dir_all(codex_home);
+    }
 
     #[test]
